items.why: Students mix up links/rechts and word order; 'links' is left and 'abbiegen' is separable.
Students don't link shops to goods; bread is bought at the Bäckerei.
Students confuse prepositions of place; 'gegenüber' means opposite, used to locate buildings.
The answer examiners want is: Opposite the station. “Inside the station” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is a building you might find in a town?
items.why: Correct answer: Das Rathaus. “Das Fahrrad” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
